column1: The earliest written records of Turkish languages date back to the 8th century CE, found in the Orkhon Inscriptions9.
The Proto-Turkic language, ancestor of all Turkic languages, is estimated to have originated in Asia around 500 BCE1.
Old Turkic, the first attested form of Turkic, has its earliest known records dating to the 7th century CE1.
The name "Türk" was first used as a political identity during the Göktürk Khaganate period in the 6th century CE7.
Tarsus is mentioned in Xenophon's Anabasis (around 400 BCE) as a great and wealthy city8.
The linguistic history of Turkic languages can be followed in written sources from the 8th century CE onward10.

This timeline demonstrates the evolution of Turkic-related terms and place names across different regions and time periods, reflecting the complex linguistic and cultural interactions throughout Eurasia.
